task_completion.description: Verify that the EColi project and FoodHistory form are loaded as the data source.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard interface should show 'EColi' and 'FoodHistory' in the data source indicator (usually located at the top of the screen or in the properties panel).

task_completion.description: Verify that the data is restricted to cases where 'Ill' equals 'Yes'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data filter should be visible in the 'Data Filters' pane (usually on the right side) showing a condition like 'Ill = Yes', 'Ill = (+)', or 'Ill = 1'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Combined Frequency gadget is created with the correct six symptoms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Combined Frequency gadget (table/chart) is visible on the dashboard canvas. The rows or variables listed must include exactly: Diarrhea, BloodyDiarrhea, Fever, Vomiting, Nausea, and AbdominalPain.

task_completion.description: Verify the gadget is titled correctly and sorted from high to low frequency.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title at the top of the gadget must be 'Symptom Profile of Cases'. The data rows or chart bars within the gadget must be ordered in descending order based on their frequency/count.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Dashboard window title bar should display 'SymptomProfile' (indicating it has been saved), or a Save dialog/File Explorer window should show 'SymptomProfile.cvs7' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Epi_Info\Dashboards\' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Epi Info 7 GUI to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard menus, adding gadgets, and setting properties, rather than just writing a script.

integrity.description: Verify the visualization is rendered by Epi Info 7.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Combined Frequency gadget should have the standard Epi Info 7 styling (e.g., blue header bar, specific table formatting with frequency/percent columns, settings gear icon) and not be a mocked-up image or generic spreadsheet.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the application by manually writing the .cvs7 XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows, PowerShell prompts, or text editors (like Notepad or VS Code) used to manually construct the XML-based .cvs7 file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Epi_Info\Dashboards\SymptomProfile.cvs7. The data source is the EColi sample project, typically located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Epi Info 7\Projects\EColi\EColi.prj. The required symptoms to include in the Combined Frequency gadget are exactly: Diarrhea, BloodyDiarrhea, Fever, Vomiting, Nausea, and AbdominalPain.
